summary: Jam‑heavy first set with stellar Crazy Fingers and a moving Morning Dew
blurb: The performance shines on Crazy Fingers, Scarlet Begonias and an emotional Morning Dew encore. Jerry’s guitar tone is crisp and his vocals mournful while Keith adds spicy organ flavor to the jams. The band stays tight throughout with strong second‑set transitions.
review:
September 30, 1976 at Ohio State’s Auditorium captures a Grateful Dead in late‑summer form. The first set builds around a scorching “Crazy Fingers” that showcases Jerry’s searing tone and a Spanish‑tinged organ weave from Keith, then rolls into a bright “Scarlet Begonias”. Highlights continue with “Peggy‑O”, “El Paso” and a heartfelt “Morning Dew” encore that leaves the hall humming. The second set offers a relaxed groove that blossoms into a soaring “Saint Stephen” jam, while “Samson & Delilah” delivers snaky lead lines from Jerry. Though a few early‑set level quirks appear on some recordings, the overall flow is smooth and the improvisations feel both tight and adventurous.

Across the available tapes the soundboard capture (gd1976-09-30.sbd.18107.shnf) stands out for its warm, cozy board tone that faithfully renders the band’s dynamics with only minor glitches. Listeners get a clear picture of Jerry’s vocal nuance, Keith’s organ flourishes, and the dual‑drum interplay, making this version the go‑to source for anyone wanting to experience one of ’76’s solid, jam‑focused nights.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings label the show as positive, praising Crazy Fingers, Scarlet Begonias and Morning Dew, while the other two are mixed but still highlight strong jams and note only recording‑quality issues. No consistent negative comments about performance; overall sentiment is moderately positive.

best_recording.reason: Good‑quality soundboard with warm, balanced board tone; captures the standout Crazy Fingers and Morning Dew encore with minimal glitches.

key_highlights: Jam‑heavy first set anchored by a scorching “Crazy Fingers”
Spicy organ textures on “Scarlet Begonias” and “Crazy Fingers”
Emotional “Morning Dew” encore that closes the night
"Saint Stephen" second‑set jam with extended improvisation

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers a crisp, mournful vocal delivery and searing guitar tone on Crazy Fingers; snaky leads shine on Samson & Delilah.
band_performance.Phil: Solid bass foundation that percolates later in the show after early level drops; holds down the groove throughout.
band_performance.Bob: Vocal crooning on El Paso praised; contributes solid rhythm guitar work across the set.
band_performance.Keith: Adds off‑the‑wall, Spanish‑flavored organ textures to Crazy Fingers and a prominent, well‑balanced presence later in the show.
band_performance.Mickey: Drumming is generally strong with a long solo in the second set that some find mixed; overall solid rhythmic support.
band_performance.Billy: Provides steady drum work alongside Mickey; occasional mix issues make his parts less distinct early on but remains reliable.
